description: Add a deterministic (no LLM) auto-summary CLI command to cli.ts that generates a summary string from git context and optional TaskMaster state, then sets it on a registered peer via the broker. Designed to be called by a SessionStart hook for immediate, reliable peer summaries without API keys.

userStories.title: Refactor cli.ts for testable exports and implement buildSummary pure function
userStories.description: As a developer, I want cli.ts refactored with an import.meta guard and a pure buildSummary function exported so that the auto-summary logic is unit-testable and the CLI entry point behavior is preserved.
acceptanceCriteria: cli.ts switch block is wrapped in `if (Bun.main === import.meta.path)` guard so importing the module does not execute CLI logic
Existing CLI commands (status, peers, send, set-name, kill-broker) still work identically after the refactor
buildSummary is exported from cli.ts with signature: buildSummary(opts: { repoName: string | null; branch: string | null; cwd: string; taskCount: number | null }): string
buildSummary with repoName='claude-peers-mcp', branch='main', cwd='/home/riche/MCPs/claude-peers-mcp', taskCount=null returns '[claude-peers-mcp:main] Working in /home/riche/MCPs/claude-peers-mcp'
buildSummary with repoName='claude-peers-mcp', branch='main', cwd='/x', taskCount=3 returns '[claude-peers-mcp:main] 3 in-progress tasks'
buildSummary with repoName=null, branch=null, cwd='/tmp/no-git', taskCount=null returns 'Working in /tmp/no-git'
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement git context helper functions
userStories.description: As a CLI command, I want helper functions getGitRepoName and getGitBranchName so that I can gather git context from the current working directory deterministically.
acceptanceCriteria: getGitRepoName(cwd) is exported from cli.ts and returns the basename of the git root directory (e.g., 'claude-peers-mcp') when called inside a git repo
getGitRepoName(cwd) returns null when called in a non-git directory without throwing
getGitBranchName(cwd) is exported from cli.ts and returns the current branch name (e.g., 'main') when called inside a git repo
getGitBranchName(cwd) returns null when called in a non-git directory without throwing
Both functions use Bun.spawnSync with stderr: 'ignore' and stdout: 'pipe'
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement TaskMaster in-progress task detection
userStories.description: As a CLI command, I want a getTaskMasterInProgress helper so that the summary can include the count of in-progress tasks when TaskMaster is available.
acceptanceCriteria: getTaskMasterInProgress(cwd) is exported from cli.ts and returns the count of in-progress tasks as a number when task-master is installed and has in-progress tasks
getTaskMasterInProgress returns null when task-master CLI is not installed (command not found)
getTaskMasterInProgress returns null when task-master list returns an error or empty result
getTaskMasterInProgress enforces a 2-second timeout using Bun.spawn with AbortSignal.timeout(2000) and returns null on timeout
No error is printed to stdout or stderr on any failure path
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Wire up the auto-summary CLI command with argument validation and help text
userStories.description: As a Claude Code SessionStart hook, I want to call `bun cli.ts auto-summary <peer-id>` so that my peer's summary is set immediately on startup without waiting for an LLM API call.
acceptanceCriteria: Running `bun cli.ts auto-summary <peer-id>` with a valid registered peer ID in a git repo sets the summary on the broker and prints the generated summary to stdout
Command exits with code 0 on success
Running `bun cli.ts auto-summary` without a peer-id prints 'Usage: bun cli.ts auto-summary <peer-id>' to stderr and exits with code 1

The command orchestrates: gathers git context (getGitRepoName + getGitBranchName), gathers TaskMaster state (getTaskMasterInProgress), builds summary (buildSummary), POSTs to broker /set-summary via brokerFetch
CLI help text (default case) includes the auto-summary command with description
Doc comment at top of cli.ts lists the auto-summary command
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Handle broker unavailability and invalid peer ID errors
userStories.description: As a hook caller, I need the auto-summary command to fail gracefully with actionable error messages when the broker is unreachable or the peer ID is invalid, so that session startup is not blocked.
acceptanceCriteria: If the broker is not reachable, the command prints 'Error: Broker not reachable at <url>. Is it running?' to stderr and exits with code 1
If the broker returns an error for /set-summary (e.g., peer not found), the error is printed to stderr and the command exits with code 1
On broker error, the generated summary is still printed to stdout for debugging purposes
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Integration and unit test suite for auto-summary
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a comprehensive test suite in cli.test.ts so that all auto-summary behavior is verified and regressions are caught.
acceptanceCriteria: cli.test.ts exists with unit tests for buildSummary covering: git+branch+tasks, git+branch no tasks, no git context, and null value combinations
cli.test.ts has integration tests that spin up a test broker on port 17899 with a temp DB (same pattern as broker.test.ts)
Integration test: register a peer, run `bun cli.ts auto-summary <peer-id>`, verify summary was set via /list-peers
Integration test: run auto-summary without peer-id argument, verify exit code 1 and usage message
Integration test: run auto-summary with nonexistent peer-id, verify exit code 1
All 19 existing tests in broker.test.ts still pass
All new tests in cli.test.ts pass via `bun test cli.test.ts`
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
